name: Random Encounters
description: A generation system that selects and places encounter content such as enemies, events, hazards, or rewards from contextual rules.
design_notes: Random encounters should feel contextual, not arbitrary. The system needs encounter_budget, encounter_tag filters, spawn safety checks, progression gates, cooldowns, and telemetry about accepted and rejected candidates.
design_purpose: Add replayable variation to travel, exploration, or mission routes.
Match encounter pressure to player progress, biome, resources, and current objectives.
Create surprises while protecting pacing, safety, and progression requirements.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world can surprise them with events that still belong to the place and moment.

parameters.name: encounter_budget
parameters.description: Budget used to choose enemy groups, hazards, or reward events.

parameters.name: encounter_tag_filter
parameters.description: Tags that constrain eligible encounter templates.

parameters.name: safe_spawn_radius_min
parameters.description: Minimum distance from the player, camera, or protected area for placement.

parameters.name: cooldown_group_id
parameters.description: Group key that prevents similar encounters from repeating too soon.

parameters.name: validation_retries
parameters.description: Attempts to find a valid template and placement before no encounter or fallback occurs.

edge_cases: All eligible encounter templates fail because player progression, biome, and safe spawn rules conflict.
An encounter spawns behind a one-way gate or inside a cleared safe zone.
Cooldowns and story flags can block every event in a small region.
A generated encounter must not kill a quest NPC, block an exit, or consume a required resource.
common_bugs: Encounter selection ignores current biome_id and spawns enemies that cannot navigate the terrain.
Rejection reasons are not logged, making empty travel segments impossible to tune.
Random encounters repeat because cooldown_group_id is saved per scene instead of per world/account scope.
Server and client choose different encounter templates because candidate ordering is nondeterministic.
balancing_notes: Encounter frequency should respect recovery resources and travel distance, not only enemy difficulty.
Surprise encounters need fairness constraints such as sightline, warning, or escape space.
Track encounter acceptance rate, player damage, and time-to-contact to tune pacing.
accessibility_notes: Avoid unavoidable surprise attacks without warning cues, especially from behind the camera.
Offer difficulty or assist options that adjust encounter_budget and frequency rather than only health values.

implementation_notes.unity: Select encounter templates from data by encounter_tag_filter, validate spawn points with NavMesh and camera checks, and log accepted/rejected candidates with seed and content_version.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep encounter tables as Resources or JSON and run placement validation before instancing scenes; save cooldown_group_id timestamps with world or account state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use EQS or PCG queries for safe spawn validation and server authority for multiplayer encounter commits; Visual Logger should capture budget, selected template, and rejection reasons.
implementation_notes.web: Use deterministic template ordering and seeded RNG streams; represent encounter commits as events so replay and bug reports can reproduce spawn choices.

example_games.title: Fallout
example_games.note: Uses travel and world encounters as part of exploration.

example_games.title: Darkest Dungeon
example_games.note: Uses encounter composition and pacing as a major run structure.
